summary:
The combination of polydactyly with craniosynostosis along with late closure of fontanelles aligns most closely with characteristics described for [Apert’s syndrome], which also presents other distinctive facial anomalies. Given these specific criteria provided about the patient presentation, it strongly indicates that this individual likely has Apert's syndrome rather than another condition listed here based primarily upon its unique constellation of symptoms including hand/foot deformities alongside skull development issues affecting multiple sutures prematurely fusing together while delaying normal infant head growth patterns due to abnormal calvarium ossification processes leading towards characteristic facies changes over time postnatal period.

Given these specific criteria provided about the patient presentation, it strongly indicates that this individual likely has Apert's syndrome rather than another condition listed here based primarily upon its unique constellation of symptoms including hand/foot deformities alongside skull development issues affecting multiple sutures prematurely fusing together while delaying normal infant head growth patterns due to abnormal calvarium ossification processes leading towards characteristic facies changes over time postnatal period.
